introduction.purpose: This document specifies the software requirements for the ICU Vitals Telemetry Gateway, which bridges bedside physiological monitors to the central hospital Electronic Health Record (EHR) system.
introduction.documentScope: This SRS covers data ingestion from bedside monitors, HL7 message formatting, and secure transmission to the EHR. It does not cover the physical hardware of the bedside monitors.

definitionsAndAcronyms.HL7: Health Level Seven International (Standards for health data exchange)

references.title: Medical Device Data Systems, Medical Image Storage Devices, and Medical Image Communications Devices

overallDescription.productPerspective: The system operates as middleware on dedicated hospital servers, sitting between the isolated bedside device network and the primary hospital IT network.
productFunctions: Continuous ingestion of heart rate, SpO2, and blood pressure data.
Conversion of proprietary device signals into standard HL7 v2.x messages.
Transmission of HL7 messages to the EHR.
userCharacteristics: No direct clinical user interface. Operated entirely by Hospital IT System Administrators.
overallDescription.operatingEnvironment: On-premise Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) 9 servers.

functionalRequirements.title: HL7 Message Construction
functionalRequirements.description: The system shall construct an HL7 ORU^R01 observation message for every complete set of vitals received.
inputs: Raw byte stream from bedside monitor IP
functionalRequirements.processingLogic: Parse byte stream to extract HR, SpO2, and BP values. Map values to corresponding LOINC codes. Construct HL7 string segments (MSH, PID, OBR, OBX).
functionalRequirements.errorHandling: If the byte stream is malformed, log a Level 2 System Error and discard the packet. Do not attempt to guess or interpolate missing clinical values.

performance: The system shall process and transmit a vitals packet to the EHR within 500 milliseconds of receipt.
reliability: The system shall experience no more than 1 dropped packet per 10,000 continuous transmissions.
availability: The gateway service shall maintain 99.999% availability.
security: All data in transit across the hospital network must be encrypted using TLS 1.3 or higher.
The system shall not store unencrypted PHI on the local disk.
maintainability: The system shall support zero-downtime rolling updates via clustering.
portability: The software must be capable of running in an air-gapped environment without internet access.

externalInterfaces.name: Bedside Monitor Serial/IP Bridge
externalInterfaces.description: Interface receiving raw telemetry data from physical patient monitors.

externalInterfaces.name: EHR Integration Engine
externalInterfaces.description: Downstream hospital record system receiving the formatted data.

assumptions: The hospital network provides a dedicated, uncontended VLAN for telemetry traffic.
Bedside monitors are calibrated and sending accurate physical data.
businessConstraints: Must be completed in time for the Q4 FDA 510(k) submission window.
technicalConstraints: System must be developed in C++17 to meet memory management predictability requirements.
regulatoryCompliance: HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act)

appendices.title: Appendix A: Traceability Matrix
appendices.description: Mapping of FR-TLM-XXX requirements to specific hazard mitigations in the risk management file.
